data.title: SLB reports Q2 2025 results, revenue at $8.55B, up 6%. Adjusted EPS at 74 cents. North America revenue up 1%, international revenue down 8%.
data.description: Schlumberger N.V. (NYSE: SLB) saw a rise in its shares following the release of its second-quarter 2025 results. Revenue for the quarter decreased by 6% year-over-year to $8.55 billion, surpassing the projected $8.47 billion. Adjusted earnings per share also fell by 13% to 74 cents, meeting analysts' predictions. In North America, revenue increased by 1% to $1.66 billion, driven by growth in data center infrastructure but offset by weaker Asset Performance Solutions (APS) revenue and a decline in U.S. land drilling activity. International revenue went down by 8% to $6.85 billion, with Latin America recording a 14% decrease due to reduced drilling in Mexico, although this was partially balanced by robust unconventional stimulation activity in Argentina.

data.title: Hire Salesforce Developers from Hyperlink InfoSystem for Customized CRM Solutions to Drive Business Growth. Leading CRM platform.
data.description: On July 18, 2025, in cities like New York and Los Angeles, Salesforce has become the top choice for businesses globally looking to enhance customer experience and streamline operations with data-driven solutions. The platform stands out as a leader in CRM, aiding in managing customer interactions, sales funnels, and overall business processes. This significant shift highlights the increasing importance of leveraging technology to excel in today's competitive market.

data.title: Sharing Hearts Fund awards grants to 5 Stark County families via Liberty HealthShare®, in collaboration with Canton Repository.
data.description: In Canton, Ohio on July 18, 2025, the Sharing Hearts Fund for Pediatric Wellness, a charitable organization affiliated with Liberty HealthShare®, and partnered with the Canton Repository, disclosed their initial disbursements to assist five Stark County families. The Sharing Hearts Fund for Pediatric Wellness aims to support the needs of children in the community.

data.title: Canadian host Simon explores advanced manufacturing at 3rd CISCE, showcasing acceleration of global supply chains.
data.description: On July 18, 2025, in Beijing, the 3rd China International Supply Chain Expo (CISCE) featured Canadian host Simon delving into the advanced manufacturing zone. This showcased the acceleration of global supply chains.

data.title: Data center management software market to hit $3.7 billion by 2030, grew from $2.2 billion in 2024, at a CAGR of 9.2%.
data.description: A report on the global Data Center Infrastructure Management Software market has been released, available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. In 2024, the market was valued at US$2.2 billion, with projections showing it could reach US$3.7 billion by 2030. This represents a projected compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 9.2% from 2024 to 2030. The report offers insights into market trends, drivers, and forecasts to aid informed decision-making.

data.title: US stocks mixed; American Express reports upbeat Q2 results. Nasdaq up 50+ points. Dow down 0.11% to 44,435.25, Nasdaq up 0.37% to 20,962.60, S&P 500 up 0.17% to 6,308.01. American Express Q2 revenue grows 9% YoY to $17.86 billion, beats estimates. Adjusted EPS $4.08, beats consensus $3.87.
data.description: U.S. stocks saw gains midway through trading, especially in the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite which rose by over 50 points on Friday. The Dow traded slightly down at 44,435.25, while the NASDAQ went up by 0.37% to 20,962.60, and the S&P 500 also rose by 0.17% to 6,308.01. Utilities shares jumped by 0.9%, but communication services stocks dipped by 0.3%. American Express Co. reported strong fiscal second-quarter 2025 results, with 9% revenue growth year-over-year to $17.86 billion, surpassing analyst estimates. The adjusted EPS of $4.08 also exceeded projections of $3.87. Telomir Pharmaceuticals, Inc. was among the equities trading up. (Character count: 601)

data.title: $9.6B Bitcoin transfer by whale, GENIUS Act audits raise correction worries in industry.
data.description: A prominent Bitcoin investor recently moved $9.6 billion in assets, causing speculation about a market correction. The GENIUS Act has introduced new regulations mandating audits for stablecoins, raising concerns within the industry.
